title: GEP Identity & Security
description: Device identity, environment fingerprinting, encryption, policy enforcement, privacy, and sanitisation

modules.name: DeviceId
modules.note: Obfuscated. Provides persistent device identity and container detection.
exports: getDeviceId(): string
isContainer(): boolean
internal_functions: _0xe5e830() — getDeviceId: reads/creates a persistent UUID from disk
_0x1daebf() — isContainer: checks /.dockerenv, cgroup, hostname patterns
_0x3af49f() — helper: compute stable hash from system attributes
_0x38838c() — helper: read cached ID from file
_0x34020e() — helper: write new ID to file

modules.name: EnvFingerprint
modules.note: Obfuscated. Captures and compares runtime environment snapshots.
exports: captureEnvFingerprint(): EnvFingerprintObject
fingerprintEnv(fp): string (SHA-256 hash of joined env fields)
envFingerprintMatch(fp1, fp2): boolean
internal_functions: _0x2daece() — main captureEnvFingerprint: reads package.json for version/client, calls getDeviceId()/isContainer()
_0x378fcf(fp) — fingerprintEnv: joins [device_id, node_version, platform, arch, os_release, evolver_version/client_version, evolver_version] with | and hashes via SHA-256, returns 8-char hex slice
_0x318d6c(fp1, fp2) — envFingerprintMatch: calls fingerprintEnv on both and compares

modules.name: Crypto
exports: generateKey(): Buffer (32 random bytes)
encrypt(plaintext: Buffer|string, key: Buffer): { ciphertext: Buffer, iv: Buffer, authTag: Buffer }
decrypt(ciphertext: Buffer, key: Buffer, iv: Buffer, authTag: Buffer): Buffer
pack({ ciphertext, iv, authTag }): Buffer (iv || authTag || ciphertext concatenated)
unpack(buf: Buffer): { iv: Buffer, authTag: Buffer, ciphertext: Buffer }
ALGORITHM, KEY_BYTES, IV_BYTES, AUTH_TAG_BYTES
internal_functions: _0x447c63() — generateKey: crypto.randomBytes(32)
_0x387816(plaintext, key) — encrypt: validates key length, generates IV, createCipheriv aes-256-gcm, returns {ciphertext, iv, authTag}
_0x5c2140(ciphertext, key, iv, authTag) — decrypt: createDecipheriv, setAuthTag, returns plaintext
_0x4715e6(parts) — pack: Buffer.concat([iv, authTag, ciphertext])
_0x4dc574(buf) — unpack: slices buf into iv[0..12], authTag[12..28], ciphertext[28..]

modules.name: Shield
modules.note: Obfuscated anti-tamper / runtime integrity module. Detects debuggers and environment drift.
exports: init(): void — capture baseline env snapshot, run debugger check
check(): void — re-run debugger detect + env drift check, set triggered flag
isTriggered(): boolean — returns whether shield has been triggered
freezeExports(obj): void — freeze all string-valued properties via Object.defineProperty (writable:false, configurable:false)
internal_functions: _0x3a28ec() — init: guards against re-init, captures env snapshot via _0xf5ede1(), calls _0x5c02bc() to check debugger
_0x30a5f9() — check: skips if not inited or already triggered; re-checks debugger via _0x5c02bc() and env drift via _0x4a6302()
_0x224be4() — isTriggered: returns _0x86d7ed boolean flag
_0x43573d(obj) — freezeExports: calls _0x1ef57d(obj)
_0x5c02bc() — detectDebugger: checks process.execArgv for --inspect/--debug flags, checks NODE_OPTIONS string, checks global.v8debug, tries require('v8').setFlagsFromString
_0x4a6302() — checkEnvDrift: compares current process.env values against baseline snapshot for watched vars (EVOLVER_SOLIDIFY_URL, A2A_HUB_URL + 2 others)
_0xf5ede1() — captureEnvSnapshot: snapshots values for the 4 watched env vars
_0x1ef57d(obj) — freezeProperties: Object.keys(obj) -> for string-type props, defineProperty writable:false configurable:false, then Object.freeze(obj)

modules.name: PolicyCheck
modules.note: Obfuscated. Enforces runtime execution policies. All exported names are obfuscated. 10+ internal functions.
inferred_responsibilities: Rule-based policy evaluation against environment fingerprint
known_internal_functions: _0x5ef2ef(a, b) — rule evaluation helper (likely: check rule against env)
_0x3b9301(a, b) — rule set builder (array-based, uses || fallback pattern)
_0x1662e4(a, b) — set intersection / filter (builds Set from array, filters)
known_callers: issueReporter.js (captureEnvFingerprint used within same call chain)

modules.name: PrivacyClient
modules.note: Plaintext. Hub privacy computing API client. Handles encrypted blob lifecycle.
exports: submitPrivacyTask(opts: {title, body?, signals?}): Promise<{taskId, status}|null>
uploadEncryptedBlob(plaintext: Buffer|string, opts: {privacyTaskId, label?}): Promise<{blobId, key, iv, authTag}|null>
executeSealedTool(opts: {toolId, blobId}): Promise<{resultKey?, resultHash?, error?}|null>
getPrivacyStatus(taskId: string): Promise<{status, progress?}|null>
getPrivacyResult(taskId: string, key: Buffer): Promise<{plaintext, resultHash?}|null>
getToolTemplates(): Promise<Array|null>
parsePrivacyParams(body: string): {toolId, blobIds[]}|null
internal_call_chains: uploadEncryptedBlob → generateKey() → encrypt(plaintext, key) → pack(parts) → fetch POST /a2a/privacy/blob/upload
getPrivacyResult → fetch GET /a2a/privacy/result/{taskId} → unpack(packed) → decrypt(ciphertext, key, iv, authTag)

modules.name: Sanitize
modules.note: Plaintext. Pre-publish payload sanitisation and leak detection.

constants.REDACT_PATTERNS: 15 regex patterns: Bearer tokens, sk- keys, GitHub tokens (ghp_/gho_/ghu_/ghs_/github_pat_), AWS AKIA keys, OpenAI sk-proj- / Anthropic sk-ant- keys, npm_ tokens, PEM private keys, basic-auth in URLs, /home/ and /Users/ paths, Windows paths, email addresses, .env file refs
constants.LEAK_SCANNERS: 14 typed scanner objects with {type, pattern, suggest} — covers api_key, github_token, npm_token, bearer_token, private_key, db_url, local_path, internal_ip, ssh_target, password, secret, basic_auth
constants.ENV_SCAN_SKIP_KEYS: Set of common system env keys to skip (PATH, HOME, SHELL, etc.)

exports: sanitizePayload(capsule: object): object — deep-clone via JSON + redactString on all string values
redactString(str: string): string — apply all REDACT_PATTERNS replacing matches with [REDACTED]
scanForLeaks(content: string): {found: boolean, leaks: [{type, value, suggestion}]}
detectEnvValueLeaks(content: string): [{type:'env_value_leak', envKey, value, suggestion}] — reverse-check process.env values in content
fullLeakCheck(content: string): {found: boolean, leaks: [...]} — combines scanForLeaks + detectEnvValueLeaks

key_relationships: EnvFingerprint depends_on DeviceId (imports getDeviceId, isContainer from baseDeviceId)
PrivacyClient depends_on Crypto (generateKey, encrypt, decrypt, pack, unpack)
PolicyCheck depends_on EnvFingerprint (captureEnvFingerprint called in policy context)
Shield monitors env vars overlapping with those tracked by EnvFingerprint
Sanitize is standalone — used by capsule publishing pipeline before hub upload
DeviceId is the identity anchor — consumed by EnvFingerprint which is consumed by PolicyCheck and IssueReporter
cross_cutting_notes: deviceId.js, envFingerprint.js, crypto.js, shield.js, policyCheck.js are all obfuscated (hex-encoded string literals + RC4-like string rotation). This is a software protection technique.
shield.js is an anti-tamper module: it detects debuggers via execArgv/NODE_OPTIONS/v8debug, monitors critical env vars for drift, and can freeze module exports.
crypto.js uses AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption — key=32B, IV=12B, authTag=16B.
sanitize.js is the only 'outbound gate' for data — applied before broadcast to hub.
privacyClient.js implements a privacy-preserving compute pattern: encrypt locally → upload ciphertext → execute sealed tool → retrieve + decrypt result.
EnvFingerprint hashes hostname and cwd to avoid leaking local paths.
source_files_analyzed: src/gep/policyCheck.js (obfuscated, IR analysis only — file too large)
